title: acmella psilocarpa
intro: Acmella psilocarpa, also known as Parlor Plant or Toothache Plant, is a tropical perennial plant native to the Americas, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The medicinal properties of Acmella psilocarpa are derived from its leaves, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for oral health issues, such as toothaches and gum inflammation. The health benefits of Acmella psilocarpa include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing and potentially alleviating symptoms of anxiety and stress. Acmella psilocarpa can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
